# Cherry — Mode Catalog

**225 modes** across 9 categories, generated by the research workflow. Each mode is one drop-in plugin implementing the [Mode ABI](ARCHITECTURE.md). Columns: **★** = channel/wow appeal (1–5), **D** = difficulty (E/M/H), **Adapt from** = an open-source source to port plus its license.

> ⚠️ License column reflects the *source to adapt*, not Cherry's shipped license. Anything non-permissive (GPL/LGPL/CC-BY-NC) is quarantined as optional, user-loaded, never bundled. See [LICENSE_POLICY](#license-policy-quick-reference).

## 1. Classic Spectral & Waveform

| # | Mode | ★ | D | What it does & how it reacts to audio | Adapt from |
|---|------|---|---|----------------------------------------|------------|
| 1 | **Circular Radial Bars** | ★★★★★ | M | FFT bars emanate radially outward from a center point, forming a starburst or sunflower that pulses in sync with frequency content. Often rendered with a mirrored outer ring. — Angle -> frequency bin; bar length -> FFT magnitude; overall radius -> RMS energy; color hue -> frequency (low=red, high=blue) | https://github.com/nicktindall/cyclon.p2p — or Shadertoy 'Audio Visualizer Circular' by FabriceNeyret2 (CC BY-NC-SA 3.0) *(CC BY-NC-SA 3.0)* |
| 2 | **Constant-Q Rainbow Spectrogram** | ★★★★★ | H | CQT (Constant-Q Transform) spectrogram where each octave spans equal vertical height. Colors cycle through the rainbow per octave, making harmonic overtone stacks visually obvious as repeating color patterns. — CQT bin -> y row with log spacing; time -> x scroll; magnitude -> brightness; octave number -> base hue (red=sub, violet=treble); harmonic series -> repeating hue pattern | https://github.com/librosa/librosa (ISC) for CQT math — render with WebGPU texture *(ISC)* |
| 3 | **Scope Tube (XY Phase Oscilloscope)** | ★★★★★ | M | Waveform samples drawn in XY mode with a simulated cathode-ray tube bloom: bright center core with soft halation, phosphor persistence trail, and beam defocus at high deflection. Stereo phase creates organic Lissajous orbits. — L -> x deflection; R -> y deflection; sample velocity -> beam brightness (faster = dimmer, CRT physics); persistence buffer -> exponential decay; mono detection -> draws diagonal line | Shadertoy 'Oscilloscope' by byt3_m3chanic (CC BY-NC-SA 3.0) *(CC BY-NC-SA 3.0)* |
| 4 | **Logarithmic FFT Bars** | ★★★★ | E | Vertical bars binned on a log-frequency axis so bass, mid, and treble occupy visually equal widths. Bars are wider on the left and narrower on the right, matching human pitch perception. — Log-spaced FFT bins -> bar height; each octave band -> equal pixel width; smoothed magnitude -> fill height with gravity-fall decay | https://github.com/karlstav/cava (MIT) *(MIT)* |
| 5 | **Polar Spectrum Ring** | ★★★★ | M | FFT magnitude is mapped to a closed polar curve drawn as a smooth spline ring, not discrete bars. The ring swells and contracts continuously, with inner glow when bass hits. — Polar angle -> frequency; radius offset from base circle -> smoothed FFT magnitude; bass bin spike -> inner bloom radius; color -> spectral centroid hue | Shadertoy 'Polar Spectrum' by mu6k (CC BY-NC-SA 3.0) *(CC BY-NC-SA 3.0)* |
| 6 | **Mirrored Waveform Tunnel** | ★★★★ | M | Waveform drawn symmetrically above and below center, then stacked in depth as receding historical frames to simulate a tunnel effect. Past waveforms fade and shrink toward the vanishing point. — Current PCM frame -> foreground waveform shape; frame history -> depth layers scaled by z; RMS -> tunnel brightness; onset -> flash on new frame push | https://github.com/ProjectM-Visualizer/projectm (LGPL-2.1) *(LGPL-2.1)* |
| 7 | **Lissajous Vectorscope** | ★★★★ | E | Left audio channel drives X, right channel drives Y, tracing Lissajous figures that reveal stereo phase and harmonic relationships. Mono signals make a diagonal line; stereo content creates orbiting ellipses or figure-eights. — L channel sample -> x coordinate; R channel sample -> y coordinate; persistence (trail length) -> inversely proportional to RMS; color brightness -> instantaneous magnitude | https://github.com/syedjafar01/Lissajous-Figures (MIT) — or from scratch *(MIT)* |
| 8 | **Spectrogram Waterfall (Horizontal)** | ★★★★ | M | Frequency runs vertically on the Y axis; time scrolls rightward as new FFT columns are appended. Color encodes magnitude using a heat-map palette (black -> blue -> yellow -> white). Reveals melodic structure and harmonics. — FFT bin index -> y pixel row; elapsed time -> x scroll position; magnitude -> color from palette LUT; bin resolution -> adjustable FFT size (1024-8192) | https://github.com/outhink/spectrogram (MIT) *(MIT)* |
| 9 | **Waterfall Cascade (Vertical Drop)** | ★★★★ | M | Newest FFT row appears at the top and previous rows fall downward, creating a cascading waterfall effect. Bass-heavy transients produce wide bright streaks that fall slowly. — Each new FFT frame -> top row of texture; rows shift downward each frame; magnitude -> luminance; spectral centroid -> hue shift across the cascade | Shadertoy 'Waterfall Spectrum' by movAX13h (CC BY-NC-SA 3.0) *(CC BY-NC-SA 3.0)* |
| 10 | **Chromagram Wheel** | ★★★★ | H | Twelve pitch classes (C through B) arranged clockwise around a circle. Each segment brightness corresponds to the chroma energy at that pitch class, revealing chord shapes as lit arc segments. — CQT chroma vector (12 bins) -> arc segment luminance; dominant pitch class -> brightest segment; chord recognition -> color-coded circle of fifths layout; onset -> segment flash | https://github.com/aubio/aubio (GPL-3.0) for chroma extraction + custom WebGL ring *(GPL-3.0)* |
| 11 | **3D Spectrum Ribbon** | ★★★★ | M | FFT bars rendered as a 3D ribbon receding into the screen, with each new frame extruding a new slice. The ribbon twists gently over time. Viewed from a low camera angle gives a dramatic landscape feel. — FFT magnitude -> ribbon vertex height at current slice; time -> z-depth extrusion; spectral centroid -> ribbon color; camera elevation -> fixed low angle; onset -> ribbon pulse | https://github.com/mrdoob/three.js examples/audio_analyser (MIT) *(MIT)* |
| 12 | **Radial Waveform Clock** | ★★★★ | M | PCM waveform is wrapped around a circle; amplitude displaces points radially. The result looks like a clock face that wobbles and breathes. A full audio buffer fits exactly one revolution. Steady drone = smooth circle; percussive hit = spiky star. — Buffer index -> angular position (0-2pi); sample amplitude -> radial offset from base radius; RMS -> base radius size; spectral centroid -> rotation speed of the ring; beat -> outer glow flash | https://github.com/mrdoob/three.js (MIT) *(MIT)* |
| 13 | **Comb Filter Harmonic Ruler** | ★★★★ | H | Fundamental frequency (detected via autocorrelation) sets a comb of harmonic markers overlaid on the log-FFT spectrum. Harmonic peaks glow when the signal energy aligns with the comb teeth, revealing overtone richness and pitch stability. — Autocorrelation peak -> fundamental Hz; harmonic series (f, 2f, 3f…) -> vertical marker positions on spectrum; FFT magnitude at each harmonic -> marker glow intensity; pitch confidence -> marker opacity; vibrato -> marker oscillation | https://github.com/aubio/aubio (GPL-3.0) for pitch detection + custom overlay *(GPL-3.0)* |
| 14 | **Stereo Split Butterfly Spectrum** | ★★★★ | M | Left channel FFT bars grow leftward from center; right channel bars grow rightward, forming a butterfly. The two wings diverge for wide stereo content and collapse together for mono. Color codes L=cyan, R=magenta with overlap in white. — L FFT magnitude -> leftward bar length; R FFT magnitude -> rightward bar length; L-R difference -> gap or overlap at center; per-bin phase coherence -> center strip color; RMS -> background vignette | from scratch using Web Audio ChannelSplitterNode + two canvas draws |
| 15 | **Linear FFT Bars** | ★★★ | E | Vertical bars arranged left-to-right on a linear frequency axis, each bar height proportional to its bin's magnitude. The classic Winamp AVS look with solid fills and optional peak dots. — FFT magnitude -> bar height; bin index -> x position (linear Hz); RMS -> overall brightness; peak hold -> detached dot y position | https://github.com/mhroth/WaveformVisualizer (MIT) or projectM (LGPL-2.1) *(LGPL-2.1)* |
| 16 | **Mirrored Spectrum** | ★★★ | E | Two log-FFT bar arrays reflected vertically from a center baseline — one growing upward, one downward — creating a butterfly symmetry that breathes with the music. — FFT magnitude -> bar extent above and below center line; low frequencies -> largest deflection; stereo L/R channels optionally mapped to up/down halves | https://github.com/ProjectM-Visualizer/projectm (LGPL-2.1) *(LGPL-2.1)* |
| 17 | **Classic Oscilloscope** | ★★★ | E | Raw PCM waveform drawn as a single continuous line sweeping left-to-right, triggered on a zero-crossing to stabilize the image. Phosphor-green glow on black background. — Sample amplitude -> y deflection; sample index -> x position; zero-crossing sync -> sweep trigger; RMS -> line brightness/glow radius | https://github.com/substack/node-oscilloscope (MIT) — or from scratch in WebGL *(MIT)* |
| 18 | **VU Meter Wall** | ★★★ | M | Array of classic analog-style VU meters tiled across the screen, each responding to a different frequency band. Needle deflection uses ballistic attack/release constants matching real VU spec (300ms integration). LEDs or needles selectable. — Per-band RMS -> needle angle; peak hold -> red segment threshold; 10-band EQ split -> 10 independent meters; overall loudness -> needle color from green through yellow to red | https://github.com/kevinkotcherga/vu-meter (MIT) *(MIT)* |
| 19 | **LED Peak Meter Columns** | ★★★ | E | Vertical columns of discrete LED segments, one per stereo channel or per frequency band. Segments light up from bottom; top red segments indicate clipping. Peak-hold segments linger before falling. — Per-channel RMS -> number of lit segments; sample peak -> momentary top segment; -6 dBFS threshold -> yellow segment start; 0 dBFS -> red clipping LED; segment decay rate -> configurable ballistics | https://github.com/karlstav/cava (MIT) — LED mode *(MIT)* |
| 20 | **Frequency Domain Heatmap Grid** | ★★★ | M | A 2D grid where X is frequency (log-spaced) and Y is time. Each cell's color encodes STFT magnitude. Unlike a standard spectrogram the grid is chunked into large square cells, giving a pixelated retro look that still shows melodic structure. — STFT magnitude -> cell color from viridis LUT; hop size -> row refresh rate; frequency bin -> column; beat onset -> row brightness boost; BPM grid overlay optional | from scratch with WebGL texture update |
| 21 | **Waveform Envelope Fill** | ★★★ | E | Full waveform history scrolls leftward; the area between the waveform and the zero line is filled with a gradient. Peak envelope is drawn as a separate brighter line above the fill. Looks like a podcast editor waveform but animated. — PCM samples -> waveform vertices; RMS windowed envelope -> fill opacity; instantaneous peak -> top line y; LF content -> warm color bias; HF content -> cool color bias | https://github.com/katspaugh/wavesurfer.js (BSD-3-Clause) *(BSD-3-Clause)* |
| 22 | **Bark-Scale Band Display** | ★★★ | M | 24 Bark critical-band filters displayed as proportionally-wide horizontal rectangles stacked vertically, mimicking the auditory filterbank. Width and brightness of each band encodes its energy, giving a perceptually-linear view of loudness. — 24 Bark band energies -> rectangle fill width; band index -> y row position; RMS per band -> fill color from dark to bright; transient sharpness -> band flash duration | from scratch — Bark filterbank coefficients are public domain |
| 23 | **Stereo Correlation Meter** | ★★★ | M | A horizontal bar ranging from -1 (out of phase) to +1 (in phase), showing the cross-correlation coefficient of L and R channels in real time. A secondary spectrogram below shows per-frequency phase coherence as a color band. — L/R cross-correlation -> needle position on -1..+1 axis; sign crossing -> warning red zone; per-bin phase difference -> color on secondary strip (green=coherent, red=opposite); RMS -> meter background glow | from scratch — standard DSP formula |
| 24 | **Spectral Centroid Tracer** | ★★★ | E | A single bright dot traces the spectral centroid frequency over time, leaving a phosphor trail. The dot moves up/down as the sound gets brighter/darker. Rapid melody changes produce swooping arcs; drones leave a flat line. — Spectral centroid (weighted mean frequency) -> y position; time -> x scroll; dot brightness -> RMS; trail decay -> exponential; spectral spread -> horizontal blur radius of trail | from scratch — centroid formula is trivial WebAudio AnalyserNode post-process |
| 25 | **Slit-Scan Waveform** | ★★★ | E | Each audio frame's waveform is rendered as a single vertical pixel column, and columns advance leftward over time — a temporal cross-section view. The result is a flowing texture that shows amplitude modulation patterns and rhythmic structure as horizontal bands. — PCM sample amplitude at each y pixel -> brightness of that pixel column; frame index -> x column position; low amplitude -> dark; clipping -> bright white flash column; RMS envelope -> column average brightness | from scratch — trivial 1D texture scroll in WebGL |

## 2. Preset-Engine Driven

| # | Mode | ★ | D | What it does & how it reacts to audio | Adapt from |
|---|------|---|---|----------------------------------------|------------|
| 1 | **Butterchurn Milkdrop Player** | ★★★★★ | M | Renders the full Butterchurn WebGL port of Milkdrop 2 inside a canvas element, cycling through thousands of community .milk presets. The preset is decoded and executed as a GLSL shader pipeline each frame, producing fluid warps, kaleidoscopes, and particle fields identical to the Winamp classic. — bass RMS -> zoom/warp speed scalar, mid-band energy -> wave_scale, treble -> motion_vector_field magnitude, onset transient -> preset beat_detect triggers warp_amount spike, full spectrum FFT -> per-vertex shader q-variables (q1..q32) feeding custom per-preset equations | https://github.com/jberg/butterchurn (MIT license); presets from https://github.com/jberg/butterchurn-presets (MIT license) *(MIT)* |
| 2 | **Milkdrop + Lyrics Overlay Hybrid** | ★★★★★ | M | Renders a Butterchurn preset as the full-screen background, then overlays synchronized LRC or WebVTT lyrics in a large semi-transparent typeface that pulses on each syllable onset. The text color and glow intensity are driven by the current frequency band, blending the retro preset aesthetic with a karaoke-style foreground layer. — butterchurn preset handles all warp/field/color, syllable timestamp -> text opacity flash (0.4->1.0 over 80ms), mid RMS -> text glow blur radius (4-20px), bass -> font scale pulse (+5%), treble -> text color hue shift | https://github.com/jberg/butterchurn (MIT) + lrc-parser (MIT) for lyrics; overlay from scratch *(MIT)* |
| 3 | **Milkdrop + Album Art Warp Hybrid** | ★★★★★ | H | Feeds the current track's album art as a custom texture into a Milkdrop preset pipeline via Butterchurn's texture injection API, so the flowing warp field morphs and distorts the artwork rather than a generated color field. The artwork is dissolved back in at chorus peaks and warps out again during quiet passages. — butterchurn per-preset warp equations distort injected album-art texture, chorus energy peak -> texture blend weight rises to 1.0, quiet passage -> blend weight fades to 0 (pure preset colors), beat onset -> warp_amount spike that shreds the image momentarily | https://github.com/jberg/butterchurn (MIT); texture injection via butterchurn setInternalMeshSize / uniform hack *(MIT)* |
| 4 | **ProjectM Preset Runner (WASM Bridge)** | ★★★★★ | H | Compiles projectM (the open-source Milkdrop engine in C++) to WebAssembly and drives it from JavaScript, giving access to the full projectM preset library and its more accurate Milkdrop 1/2 compatibility than Butterchurn. Audio PCM and FFT are passed via SharedArrayBuffer to the WASM module each frame. — PCM waveform -> projectM internal waveform buffer, FFT spectrum -> projectM spectrum buffer, all audio reactivity handled inside preset .milk equations (same mapping as Butterchurn mode but more accurate to original) | https://github.com/projectM-visualizer/projectm (LGPL-2.1); Emscripten build targets already exist in repo *(LGPL-2.1)* |
| 5 | **Shadertoy Preset Carousel** | ★★★★★ | M | Hosts a curated list of audio-reactive Shadertoy shaders (fetched via the Shadertoy API or embedded as GLSL strings) and cycles through them like a preset engine. The mode wraps each shader's iChannel0 (FFT texture) and iChannel1 (waveform texture) with the live audio data, dropping in community shaders as 'presets' with zero extra code. — FFT magnitude array -> iChannel0 (256x1 R8 texture, standard Shadertoy audio format), waveform PCM -> iChannel1 (256x1 R8 texture), iTime -> cumulative playhead seconds, beat -> custom uniform u_beat (0/1 impulse), bass RMS -> u_bass float | https://www.shadertoy.com/results?query=&sort=popular&filter=soundinput (CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 typical); wrapper from scratch *(CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 (per shader))* |
| 6 | **Preset Morph Blender (Dual-Pipeline)** | ★★★★★ | H | Runs two Butterchurn instances simultaneously in separate offscreen framebuffers, then blends them with a transition shader (dissolve, pixel-sort, radial wipe) whose progress is driven by audio energy. High energy pushes toward preset B; low energy drifts back to preset A, creating a continuous audio-reactive crossfade between two living presets. — RMS loudness -> blend alpha between framebuffer A and B (loud=B dominant), onset -> start transition animation, BPM -> lock transition speed to beat grid, spectral flux -> choose which of 6 transition shader types to apply (dissolve/wipe/pixelsort/blur/scanline/checkerboard) | https://github.com/jberg/butterchurn (MIT); transition shaders from gl-transitions (MIT) https://github.com/gl-transitions/gl-transitions *(MIT)* |
| 7 | **Milkdrop Song-Section Preset Playlist** | ★★★★★ | H | Uses a machine-learning section detector (Essentia.js or Meyda segment analysis) to identify intro/verse/chorus/bridge/outro in real time, then maps each section label to a specific Milkdrop preset from a playlist JSON. On section change a Butterchurn blend transition fires, so the visual arc of the song is pre-composed like a music video. — MFCC sequence change detection -> section boundary event -> blendPreset with playlist entry, within-section bass -> warp_speed, within-section treble -> motion_vectors, RMS -> zoom, onset -> wave_scale spike; section type (chorus) -> prefer high-energy presets via tag metadata in playlist JSON | https://github.com/jberg/butterchurn (MIT); Meyda.js (MIT) https://github.com/meyda/meyda for segment features *(MIT)* |
| 8 | **Preset Roulette: Genre-Tagged Auto-DJ** | ★★★★★ | H | Maintains a tagged preset library where each .milk file is annotated with genre tags (ambient, edm, metal, jazz, classical). A real-time genre classifier (trained on MFCCs) identifies the playing track's genre and loads the matching preset sub-library, then auto-DJs through it beat-by-beat. Genre mismatch triggers an immediate preset swap. — MFCC vectors (40 coefficients, 1-second window) -> genre classifier (TensorFlow.js k-NN or small CNN) -> preset sub-library selection, beat -> advance within sub-library, spectral flux variance (high=energetic) -> prefer high-complexity presets in sub-library, RMS -> global warp_speed scale within Butterchurn | https://github.com/jberg/butterchurn (MIT); Meyda.js (MIT) for MFCCs; TensorFlow.js (Apache 2.0) for classifier; preset tags from community annotation *(MIT / Apache 2.0)* |
| 9 | **Butterchurn Curated Playlist Mode** | ★★★★ | M | Extends the basic Butterchurn player with a hand-curated JSON playlist that maps song sections (intro, verse, chorus, bridge, outro) to specific named presets. Smooth cross-fade blending (Butterchurn blendPreset API) occurs at detected section boundaries, so the visual narrative follows the musical structure. — section-boundary onset (large energy drop or rise over 2-4 bars) -> trigger blendPreset with next curated preset, beat RMS -> warp_speed within current preset, spectral centroid -> modulate hue_rotation q-var, low-frequency power -> zoom_amount | https://github.com/jberg/butterchurn (MIT); custom playlist JSON layer from scratch *(MIT)* |
| 10 | **Butterchurn Beat-Reactive Preset Switcher** | ★★★★ | E | Runs Butterchurn continuously but switches to a randomly selected preset on every N-th detected beat, using Butterchurn's built-in cross-fade blending. The switchover threshold adapts to BPM so faster tracks flip more frequently, creating a non-stop kaleidoscope of community presets locked to the pulse. — beat onset -> countdown to preset switch (every 8 or 16 beats configurable), beat strength -> blend transition speed (hard cut vs slow dissolve), BPM estimate -> switch frequency, bass power -> persist current preset if energy is very high | https://github.com/jberg/butterchurn (MIT); beat detection via Meyda.js (MIT) *(MIT)* |
| 11 | **AVS Classic Stack Emulator** | ★★★★ | H | Emulates Winamp's Advanced Visualization Studio (AVS) layered render pipeline in WebGL: a configurable stack of effect components (oscilloscope, superscope, dot-plane, blitter feedback, color map) that are composited in order each frame, matching the APE plug-in architecture of the original. — waveform PCM -> oscilloscope/superscope x-y path, beat -> blitter-feedback alpha pulse, spectrum bands -> dot-plane Z-height field, RMS -> global brightness scale, onset -> color-map cycle trigger | https://github.com/visbot/webvsc (MIT, AVS-to-WebGL converter); avs-preset-viewer projects on GitHub *(MIT)* |
| 12 | **GeISS-Style Feedback Warp** | ★★★★ | M | Implements the GeISS visualizer feedback loop: each frame the previous frame is scaled, rotated, and color-shifted by a tiny amount (the 'warp'), then new waveform geometry is drawn on top. The accumulation over hundreds of frames creates the signature smeared, spiraling trails that made GeISS legendary. — bass RMS -> warp rotation angle delta (0-2 deg/frame), treble -> scale factor tweak (0.995-1.005), mid energy -> color-shift rate (hue rotation per frame), waveform PCM -> line geometry drawn each frame, onset -> sudden warp jump (angle += 15 deg) | from scratch; GeISS algorithm well-documented in Ryan Geiss's own write-up; WebGL framebuffer ping-pong pattern |
| 13 | **Milkdrop Custom Waveform Overlay** | ★★★★ | M | Runs a Butterchurn preset underneath but replaces the preset's built-in waveform rendering with a custom WebGL waveform drawn in a separate pass (thick ribbon, 3D helix, or neon oscilloscope) blended on top. This lets any preset gain a visually distinct waveform regardless of what the preset author specified. — butterchurn handles all field/warp audio mapping, PCM waveform -> custom ribbon geometry (x=sample index, y=amplitude), RMS -> ribbon width and opacity, spectral centroid -> ribbon color hue, onset -> ribbon emits burst of particles that drift away | https://github.com/jberg/butterchurn (MIT); custom waveform renderer from scratch (WebGL lines) *(MIT)* |
| 14 | **Plane9 Scene Import Mode** | ★★★★ | H | Adapts Plane9 scene files (XML + GLSL) as importable presets, re-implementing the Plane9 scene graph in WebGL: each scene is a DAG of textured quads with per-node GLSL shaders and audio-parameter bindings defined in XML. Users drop in .p9 scene files from the community library and they run as-is. — Plane9 XML audio-binding attributes map bass/mid/treble/beat to named float uniforms (amp_bass, amp_mid, amp_hi, beat01) which each scene's shaders consume for scale, rotation, opacity, color shift; PCM waveform -> waveform texture uniform | Plane9 scene format documentation (proprietary scenes, but format reverse-engineered); WebGL scene graph from scratch; scenes from https://www.plane9.com/scenes (freeware) *(N/A (freeware scenes, custom renderer from scratch))* |
| 15 | **MilkDrop Reaction-Diffusion Preset Hybrid** | ★★★★ | H | Injects a Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion simulation as the per-frame texture feed for a Butterchurn preset instead of the normal feedback buffer. Audio parameters modulate the RD feed (F) and kill (k) rates, making the chemical pattern's topology change with the music, while the Milkdrop warp equations distort the RD texture in real time. — bass RMS -> Gray-Scott F (feed rate, 0.01-0.08), treble -> k (kill rate, 0.04-0.07) shifting pattern between spots/stripes/holes, mid energy -> diffusion coefficient, onset -> localized chemical injection (100px radius blob of reactant A), beat -> warp_amount spike distorting RD texture | https://github.com/jberg/butterchurn (MIT); RD simulation from https://www.shadertoy.com/view/lld3z8 (CC BY-NC-SA 3.0) adapted to ping-pong FBO *(MIT + CC BY-NC-SA 3.0)* |
| 16 | **NSS / WhiteCap Style Organic Blob Presets** | ★★★★ | H | Implements a preset engine modeled on SoundSpectrum's WhiteCap / G-Force organic blob aesthetic: glossy 3D blobs, ribbon worms, and crystalline spikes rendered with environment-mapped WebGL meshes. A preset file defines which primitive types and color themes to combine, and the engine morphs between preset JSON configs on beat boundaries. — bass RMS -> blob radius and scale, mid-band harmonic content (spectral spread) -> spike count and sharpness, treble -> environment map rotation speed, onset -> emit new worm ribbon from center, BPM -> blob oscillation frequency locks to tempo, spectral centroid -> environment map color temperature | from scratch (proprietary original); WebGL env-map sphere primitives, ribbon geometry; color themes from community JSON |
| 17 | **Electric Sheep Flam3 Fractal Preset Player** | ★★★★ | H | Renders Electric Sheep / flam3 fractal flame animations as presets, playing back pre-rendered sheep genomes or generating new ones via flam3-WASM. Each sheep genome is a 'preset' that the engine cross-fades between on song section changes, with audio modulating the transform weights to make the fractal breathe with the music. — bass -> chaos (variation weight on first transform), treble -> zoom level, mid energy -> color speed (palette rotation rate), onset -> trigger genome blend to next sheep, RMS -> brightness/gamma of log-density histogram, beat -> camera rotation angle step | https://github.com/scottdraves/flam3 (GPL-2.0); WASM port attempt at https://github.com/nicktindall/cyclon.p2p-rtc-client (unrelated — use flam3 directly); genome library from electricsheep.org (CC BY 3.0) *(GPL-2.0 (flam3); CC BY 3.0 (genomes))* |
| 18 | **Milkdrop Composite: Spectrum Bars + Preset Background** | ★★★★ | E | Composites a classic spectrum analyzer bar display (sharp or rounded bars, linear or logarithmic scale) as a semi-transparent foreground layer over a full-screen Butterchurn preset background. The bars are drawn in WebGL with per-bar color derived from the preset's current dominant hue, unifying the two layers visually. — butterchurn handles all background animation driven by full audio, FFT magnitude per-bin -> bar height (logarithmic scale, 64 bars), per-bar hue sampled from butterchurn output texture center row for color harmony, onset -> bar glow pulse (additive bloom pass), RMS -> overall bar opacity (quiet=more transparent revealing preset) | https://github.com/jberg/butterchurn (MIT); spectrum bar renderer from scratch (WebGL instanced quads) *(MIT)* |
| 19 | **AVS Superscope Playground** | ★★★ | H | Exposes only the AVS Superscope component as a standalone mode: users write or load expression-language formulas (same syntax as original AVS) that map audio variables (b, v, t, w, h) to point x/y/r/g/b/a, plotting thousands of colored dots per frame. Community superscope presets drop in directly. — b (bass 0-1) and v (spectrum value at current index) are built-in AVS audio variables passed per-point; t (time) modulates animation; onset -> trigger variable resets; formula output x,y -> screen position, r,g,b -> color | https://github.com/grandchild/vis_avs (GPL-2.0); expression parser subset from scratch or adapt jexl (MIT) *(GPL-2.0 (AVS source); MIT for expression parser layer)* |
| 20 | **XMMS/Infinity Plugin Replica** | ★★★ | M | Ports the Infinity plugin (shipped with XMMS/BMP on Linux) which runs a stack of 8-16 layered 'effect' passes — blur, wave, particle sprinkle, and color cycle — on a shared framebuffer. Its look is softer and more abstract than Milkdrop: pastel blobs blooming and fading over long decay. — spectrum low bins -> blob radius, mid bins -> blob spawn rate, treble -> sparkle particle count, RMS -> global fade rate (slow at low energy keeps trails longer), beat -> hue cycle jump of 60 degrees | https://github.com/hartwork/infinity-plugin (GPL-2.0); port algorithm to WebGL canvas *(GPL-2.0)* |
| 21 | **BeatDrop AVS-Style Color Map Cycler** | ★★★ | E | Implements the AVS Color Map effect as a standalone mode: a 256-entry gradient palette is mapped to luminance values on the rendered waveform/blob canvas. On each beat a new gradient is cross-faded in from a community palette library, cycling colors without changing geometry — the AVS signature 'mood ring' effect. — luminance of waveform geometry -> palette index lookup (0-255), beat onset -> begin 500ms cross-fade to next palette, BPM -> palette cycle rate, treble energy -> shift palette offset (scroll colors without switching), bass -> contrast/gamma applied to luminance before palette lookup | AVS color-map logic from https://github.com/grandchild/vis_avs (GPL-2.0); palette library from lospec.com (public domain palettes) *(GPL-2.0)* |
| 22 | **libvisual Plugin Host (WASM)** | ★★★ | H | Compiles libvisual (the Linux visualization abstraction layer) and several of its classic actor plugins (jakdaw, corona, madspin, oinksie) to WASM, hosting them as selectable 'presets' in the mode. Audio PCM and spectrum are fed through the libvisual C API, giving access to the entire Linux desktop plugin ecosystem in-browser. — PCM samples -> libvisual audio input buffer (mono, 44100 Hz), FFT spectrum -> libvisual spectrum buffer, all internal audio-to-visual mapping handled per plugin (each actor has its own equations), beat -> libvisual beat event, RMS -> energy event | https://github.com/libvisual/libvisual (LGPL-2.1); Emscripten port experimental *(LGPL-2.1)* |
| 23 | **Frei0r Plugin Preset Stack** | ★★★ | H | Chains multiple Frei0r video effect plugins (blur, lens distortion, glow, pixelize, vignette) in a processing stack driven by audio, with the stack topology and parameter values saved as 'presets'. Community Frei0r chain presets can be imported as JSON, instantly creating new visual styles without shader coding. — bass RMS -> lens_distortion amount parameter, treble -> glow threshold parameter, mid -> pixelize block_size (quantized), onset -> vignette radius pulse inward, beat -> chain reorder shuffle (swap two effects in stack), spectrum centroid -> hue rotation in color_only plugin | https://github.com/dyne/frei0r (GPL-2.0); compile plugins to WASM via Emscripten or use JS reimplementations *(GPL-2.0)* |
| 24 | **MilkDrop Minimal (Waveform-Only Presets)** | ★★★ | E | Filters the preset library to only those Milkdrop presets that use only waveform/oscilloscope geometry and no complex pixel shaders, then renders them via a lightweight software renderer (no WebGL dependency). The result is a retro Winamp-circa-2001 look: clean line art oscilloscopes on black with color cycling, running even on low-powered devices. — PCM waveform -> oscilloscope path x,y geometry, RMS -> line brightness, beat -> color cycle step, spectral centroid -> waveform color hue, zero-crossing rate -> line thickness (thicker for noisier signal), onset -> brief white flash background | Subset of https://github.com/jberg/butterchurn-presets (MIT); lightweight 2D canvas renderer from scratch *(MIT)* |
| 25 | **OpenMPT / ModPlug Tracker Scope View Presets** | ★★★ | M | For tracker-format audio (MOD/XM/IT), renders each channel's oscilloscope in a grid layout with the channel's note/instrument name overlaid, mimicking the OpenMPT pattern editor scope view. A preset library defines grid layouts, color themes, and scope styles (line, filled, dots) that can be switched on-the-fly. — per-channel PCM buffer -> individual oscilloscope geometry (x=sample, y=amplitude), channel volume -> scope height scale, channel panning -> scope x-offset in grid cell, note pitch -> scope line color (mapped through chromatic color wheel), pattern row tick -> flash column header | https://github.com/OpenMPT/openmpt (BSD-3-Clause) for tracker playback; scope renderer from scratch *(BSD-3-Clause)* |

## 3. Demoscene & Shader Effects

| # | Mode | ★ | D | What it does & how it reacts to audio | Adapt from |
|---|------|---|---|----------------------------------------|------------|
| 1 | **Mandelbulb Raymarch** | ★★★★★ | H | Raymarched 3-D Mandelbulb fractal. Camera slowly orbits; surface detail and glow intensity react to audio. Ambient occlusion and specular highlights give volumetric depth. — bass RMS -> power parameter (7-9, changes bulb shape); mid energy -> orbit-trap colour weight; beat -> camera jolt / zoom-in pulse; high freq -> glow threshold | Shadertoy 'Mandelbulb' by duke (https://www.shadertoy.com/view/MdXSWn) — CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 *(CC BY-NC-SA 3.0)* |
| 2 | **Kaleido IFS Mirror** | ★★★★★ | H | Kaleidoscopic IFS (folding mirrors in 3-D) produces infinite crystalline geometry. Colour bands shift chromatically with spectrum. — spectral centroid -> fold-angle offset (0-PI/6); bass -> zoom speed into fractal; mid band -> symmetry order (6/8/12); beat -> colour palette swap | Shadertoy 'Kali IFS' by Kali (https://www.shadertoy.com/view/ls2GRz) — CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 *(CC BY-NC-SA 3.0)* |
| 3 | **Gyroid SDF Scene** | ★★★★★ | H | Raymarched triply-periodic minimal surface (gyroid, g(x,y,z) = sin x cos y + sin y cos z + sin z cos x = 0). Camera tunnels through the lattice, lit with subsurface-scatter approximation. — bass -> gyroid frequency (scale of lattice cells); mid -> camera speed through tunnels; beat -> emission pulse (surface briefly glows); spectral flux -> surface colour hue rotation | Shadertoy 'Gyroid' by Shane (https://www.shadertoy.com/view/4tSGRz) — CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 *(CC BY-NC-SA 3.0)* |
| 4 | **Mandelbox Fly-Through** | ★★★★★ | H | Raymarched Mandelbox (a box-fold/sphere-fold variant of Mandelbrot). Generates architectural corridor-like interiors with infinite recursive detail. Ambient occlusion only, no hard shadows, for real-time performance. — bass -> scale parameter (2.0-3.0, changes corridor vs. spiky geometry); mid energy -> orbit-trap colour contribution; beat -> sudden camera teleport to random sub-structure; loudness -> AO intensity | Shadertoy 'Mandelbox' by Bers (https://www.shadertoy.com/view/XdXGW8) — CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 *(CC BY-NC-SA 3.0)* |
| 5 | **Fractal Flame (IFS Attractor)** | ★★★★★ | H | Iterated Function System attractor rendered with histogram density estimation (the Electric Sheep / flam3 technique). Millions of chaotic orbit points accumulate into vivid fractal flames. — bass -> affine transform weights (morphs flame shape); mid RMS -> colour speed along palette; beat -> variation swap (sinusoidal <-> spherical IFS variation); high freq -> gamma / brightness | flam3 (https://github.com/scottdraves/flam3) — GPL-2.0; consider GPU port via CUDA/WebGL compute *(GPL-2.0)* |
| 6 | **Tunnel Warp** | ★★★★ | E | Infinite textured tunnel rendered via UV polar mapping. The viewer flies forward or backward through a patterned cylinder, with walls morphing to the beat. — bass -> tunnel speed (forward velocity); mid energy -> rotation rate; beat onset -> radial pulse that briefly widens/narrows the tunnel; high freq -> texture tile frequency | Shadertoy 'Tunnel' by iq (https://www.shadertoy.com/view/Ms2SWW) — MIT / CC0 *(CC0)* |
| 7 | **Menger Sponge IFS** | ★★★★ | H | Iterated-function-system Menger sponge rendered with distance-estimated raymarching. Reflective surfaces and edge glow pulse to rhythm. — beat onset -> iteration count spike (3->5) causing detail burst; bass -> scale/fold modulation; mid -> reflection colour hue; overall loudness -> fog density | Shadertoy 'Menger Journey' by knighty (https://www.shadertoy.com/view/Mdf3Rr) — CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 *(CC BY-NC-SA 3.0)* |
| 8 | **Starfield Warp** | ★★★★ | E | Procedural star-field with logarithmic depth. Stars stream toward the viewer (warp speed) or scatter outward on beat. Depth-of-field blur at close range. — bass -> warp speed multiplier; beat onset -> sudden speed burst then decay; high freq energy -> star spawn density; spectral centroid -> star colour temperature (blue-white vs. red) | Shadertoy 'Warp Speed' by Jorge Lined (https://www.shadertoy.com/view/Msl3Rr) — CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 *(CC BY-NC-SA 3.0)* |
| 9 | **Voxel Landscape** | ★★★★ | M | Comanche-style voxel height-map terrain fly-through. A 2-D height texture is ray-cast in screen-space columns. Mountains and valleys scroll past as the camera advances. — bass -> terrain amplitude scale (mountains grow); mid -> fly speed; beat -> camera altitude dip (dive into valley); high freq -> fog distance and sky colour shift | Shadertoy 'Voxel Landscape' by Dave Hoskins (https://www.shadertoy.com/view/4ds3zn) — CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 *(CC BY-NC-SA 3.0)* |
| 10 | **Domain-Warp Noise Field** | ★★★★ | M | Layered fbm noise with domain warping (q = fbm(p + fbm(p))). Produces flowing, organic ink-in-water patterns. Colour is mapped from noise output via palette LUT. — bass -> warp amplitude (distortion strength); mid -> time speed multiplier; beat -> secondary warp injection pulse; spectral centroid -> palette LUT position | Shadertoy 'Warping' by iq (https://www.shadertoy.com/view/lsl3RH) — CC0 *(CC0)* |
| 11 | **Reaction-Diffusion (Gray-Scott)** | ★★★★ | H | Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion system simulated on GPU ping-pong buffers. Patterns self-organise into spots, stripes, and labyrinthine mazes. Colour encodes chemical concentration. — bass -> feed rate F (drives spot vs. stripe morphology); mid -> kill rate k; beat onset -> localised concentration injection (draws new pattern seed); high freq -> diffusion rate ratio | pmneila/JavaScript-Reaction-Diffusion on GitHub (MIT) — port WebGL buffers to target renderer *(MIT)* |
| 12 | **Audio-Reactive Julia Set** | ★★★★ | E | Classic complex-plane Julia set iterated in real time. The c-parameter orbits a curve, continuously morphing the fractal shape. Colour is escape-time based with smooth banding. — bass -> real part of c (shape topology); mid -> imaginary part of c; beat -> zoom-in burst toward a filament; high freq -> max iteration count (more detail at high energy) | Shadertoy 'Julia Set' by iq (https://www.shadertoy.com/view/MdXSWn) — CC0 *(CC0)* |
| 13 | **Neon Grid / Tron Plane** | ★★★★ | E | Infinite reflective grid floor extending to the horizon, lit by a glowing sky strip. Perspective-correct line rendering with bloom. Beat causes shockwave rings to emanate from the camera. — beat -> shockwave ring emit (radial UV distortion, decays over ~0.5s); bass -> grid line glow width; mid -> horizon sky-stripe hue; high freq -> reflection blur intensity | Shadertoy 'Grid' by iq (https://www.shadertoy.com/view/XdBGzd) — CC0 *(CC0)* |
| 14 | **Psychedelic Feedback Loop** | ★★★★ | E | Previous frame is fed back into the shader scaled, rotated, and hue-shifted, producing an infinite zoom-and-rotate colour tunnel from prior frames. The evolving history creates hypnotic trails. — bass -> feedback zoom factor (>1.0 expands, <1.0 contracts); mid -> rotation angle per frame; beat -> hue shift injection; high freq -> saturation boost of fed-back texture | Shadertoy 'Feedback' by flockaroo (https://www.shadertoy.com/view/MdfSzs) — CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 *(CC BY-NC-SA 3.0)* |
| 15 | **Hyperbolic Tiling** | ★★★★ | H | Poincare disc model hyperbolic plane tiled with {4,5} or {5,4} polygon tessellations. Conformal Mobius transforms animate the viewpoint, making tiles flow toward the disc edge. — bass -> Mobius translation speed (camera drift through hyperbolic space); mid -> tile colouring scheme rotation; beat -> discrete jump to adjacent tile; high freq -> tile edge glow intensity | Shadertoy 'Hyperbolic Tiling' by mla (https://www.shadertoy.com/view/MstcDs) — CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 *(CC BY-NC-SA 3.0)* |
| 16 | **SDF Morphing Primitives** | ★★★★ | M | A set of SDF primitives (sphere, torus, box, capsule) smoothly blend (smin) from one to another in sequence. Physically-based shading with an HDRI sky approximation. — bass -> smooth-min blend radius (k factor, controls sharpness of merge); mid -> morph progress interpolation speed; beat -> jump to next shape pair; high freq -> specular roughness | Shadertoy 'SDF Morphing' by iq (https://www.shadertoy.com/view/Xds3zN) — CC0 *(CC0)* |
| 17 | **Plasma Sine Field** | ★★★ | E | Classic demoscene plasma: overlapping sine waves produce smoothly morphing colour blobs across the screen. Palette cycles through hue as the music evolves. — bass amplitude -> wave frequency multiplier; mid RMS -> colour palette speed; onset/beat -> phase-shift spike; high-freq energy -> number of sine layers (2-6) | Shadertoy 'Plasma' by iq (https://www.shadertoy.com/view/ldBGRR) — public domain / CC0 *(CC0)* |
| 18 | **Metaball Soup** | ★★★ | M | Organic metaballs (Blinn implicit surfaces) floating and merging in 3-D space. Smooth blending gives a liquid, biological appearance. — bass -> blob radius scale; mid energy -> number of active blobs (3-12); beat -> impulse velocity kick to nearest blob; high freq -> surface shininess / specular exponent | Shadertoy 'Metaballs' by iq (https://www.shadertoy.com/view/ld2GRz) — CC0 *(CC0)* |
| 19 | **Procedural Fire** | ★★★ | E | Fragment-shader fire simulation using domain-warped noise layers scrolling upward. Flame colour shifts from deep red at base to bright yellow-white at tips. — bass RMS -> flame height multiplier; mid energy -> turbulence scale; beat -> intensity burst (brightness spike); overall loudness -> fire width | Shadertoy 'Fire' by iq (https://www.shadertoy.com/view/XsXSWS) — CC0 *(CC0)* |
| 20 | **Voronoi Crackle** | ★★★ | M | Animated Voronoi diagram with cell boundaries emitting light. Cells shift, merge and split. Interior colour is per-cell and tied to band energy in that frequency bucket. — per-cell colour hue -> corresponding sub-band energy (cells mapped to 16 frequency bins); bass -> cell site movement speed; beat -> random re-seed of site positions; overall loudness -> edge glow width | Shadertoy 'Voronoi' by iq (https://www.shadertoy.com/view/ldl3Dl) — CC0 *(CC0)* |
| 21 | **Bokeh Light Field** | ★★★ | M | Hundreds of soft circular bokeh discs float in 3-D depth layers, drifting upward. Each disc's colour and size respond to audio. Produces a dreamy, concert-lighting atmosphere. — bass -> large background disc radius; mid -> medium-layer disc brightness; beat -> simultaneous flash of all discs then fade; high freq -> small foreground disc spawn rate | Shadertoy 'Bokeh' by marius (https://www.shadertoy.com/view/4d2Xzw) — CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 *(CC BY-NC-SA 3.0)* |
| 22 | **Lissajous Surface Warp** | ★★★ | M | 3-D parametric Lissajous curves swept into a surface mesh, rendered as a glowing wireframe. Frequency ratios between axes create knotted, toroidal shapes. — bass -> X-axis frequency integer (1-5); mid -> Y-axis frequency integer (1-5); high -> Z-axis frequency; beat -> phase offset jump producing sudden knot re-tie; loudness -> glow bloom radius | from scratch (parametric surface + line rendering, ~80 lines GLSL) |
| 23 | **Spherical Harmonic Blob** | ★★★ | M | A sphere displaced by real spherical harmonics of varying order. The coefficients animate to music, making the sphere grow spikes, flatten, or twist into organic shapes. — per-harmonic-band coefficient -> corresponding sub-band energy (l=0 DC, l=1 bass, l=2 low-mid, etc.); beat -> sudden coefficient randomisation; loudness -> displacement scale | Shadertoy 'Spherical Harmonics' by iq (https://www.shadertoy.com/view/lsfXWH) — CC0 *(CC0)* |
| 24 | **Cellular Automaton (Cyclic CA)** | ★★★ | M | Cyclic cellular automaton (Greenberg-Hastings or Brian's Brain rules) evolves on GPU ping-pong texture. Spiral waves and particle cascades self-organise across the grid. — beat -> scatter random live cells seeding new wave fronts; bass -> evolution speed (steps per frame); mid energy -> number of states N (3-16, changes spiral complexity); loudness -> grid cell colour palette | Shadertoy 'Cyclic Automaton' by tdhooper (https://www.shadertoy.com/view/4sBXWt) — CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 *(CC BY-NC-SA 3.0)* |
| 25 | **Rotozoomer** | ★★ | E | Full-screen texture rotates and zooms continuously via affine UV transform. Texture is a generated procedural pattern (checkers, Truchet tiles, or plasma). A demoscene staple. — bass -> zoom rate; mid -> rotation speed; beat -> direction-reversal snap; high freq -> texture frequency (zoom into finer detail) | from scratch (trivial affine UV shader, ~20 lines GLSL) |

## 4. Particle & Physics Systems

| # | Mode | ★ | D | What it does & how it reacts to audio | Adapt from |
|---|------|---|---|----------------------------------------|------------|
| 1 | **Curl-Noise Particle River** | ★★★★★ | H | Tens of thousands of GPU particles flow continuously through a 3D curl-noise vector field. The field itself breathes and twists in time with the music, creating organic river-like tendrils that never self-intersect. — bass energy -> curl-noise octave amplitude (field turbulence intensity); mid-band power -> advection speed multiplier; onset -> random seed jump that reorganizes the field; spectral flux -> noise frequency scale | https://github.com/dli/fluid (MIT) — port GLSL curl-noise advection to WebGL2 transform feedback *(MIT)* |
| 2 | **Gravity-Well Galaxy** | ★★★★★ | H | GPU point-sprite particles orbit multiple gravity wells whose mass and position are driven by frequency bands. Particles trail long additive-blend tails, creating glowing spiral-arm structures reminiscent of galaxies. — sub-bass band power -> central well mass (controls orbital tightness); each of 4-8 frequency bands -> mass of corresponding satellite well; BPM-synced timer -> well orbital radius pulsing; onset -> sudden well position jump | https://github.com/hughsk/particle-gravity (MIT) — rewrite compute shader in WebGL2 GPGPU ping-pong textures *(MIT)* |
| 3 | **Audio-Displaced Point Cloud** | ★★★★★ | M | A dense static point cloud (loaded from a PLY mesh or generated on a sphere/plane) has each vertex displaced outward along its normal by the audio spectrum amplitude at the corresponding frequency bin, creating a spiky, reactive sculpture. — FFT bin N -> displacement of vertex N (or vertex cluster N); overall RMS -> global scale; onset -> brief shockwave ripple across the mesh; LFO synced to BPM -> slow rotation speed | https://github.com/mrdoob/three.js/blob/dev/examples/webgl_points_waves.html (MIT) — replace sine wave with FFT displacement *(MIT)* |
| 4 | **GPU Galaxy N-Body** | ★★★★★ | H | True GPU N-body simulation with 64k-256k point-mass particles using WebGL2 GPGPU ping-pong textures. Initial conditions form a disk galaxy; audio perturbations inject energy and alter the gravitational softening parameter, causing the galaxy to heat, cool, and form bars. — bass -> gravitational softening epsilon (small epsilon = sharp dense core); treble -> particle temperature (random velocity injection); onset -> spawn a second galaxy offset that will merge; BPM-locked timer -> periodic spiral density wave injection | https://github.com/Erkaman/gl-nbody (MIT) — replace fixed timestep with audio-reactive parameter injection *(MIT)* |
| 5 | **Smoke Plume Simulation** | ★★★★★ | H | A 2D grid-based Eulerian fluid simulation renders smoke plumes rising from audio-reactive sources. Velocity and density fields are computed on CPU or via WebGL2, and the smoke color shifts through a spectrum that tracks the tonal quality of the audio. — RMS -> smoke density injected per frame at source; bass -> buoyancy force (how fast smoke rises); onset -> vortex confinement spike that curls the plume dramatically; spectral rolloff -> temperature gradient coloring (warm bright base, cool dark top); stereo -> dual asymmetric sources | https://github.com/PavelDoGreat/WebGL-Fluid-Simulation (MIT) — restrict to smoke mode, add audio injection points *(MIT)* |
| 6 | **Lissajous Particle Spray** | ★★★★★ | M | Particles are emitted along Lissajous curve paths defined by left and right audio channel frequencies. The spray density and deviation around each curve track amplitude, creating neon wire-sculpture shapes that evolve with the stereo field. — left channel dominant frequency -> X oscillation frequency ratio; right channel dominant frequency -> Y oscillation frequency ratio; amplitude L/R -> particle spray deviation from curve; phase difference L/R -> phase offset delta in Lissajous; RMS -> emitter rate | https://github.com/meatfighter/oscilloscope-music-player (MIT) — add particle spray around the line path *(MIT)* |
| 7 | **Reaction-Diffusion Particle Seeding** | ★★★★★ | H | A Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion system runs on the GPU. Audio-reactive particle emitters drop activator chemical into the field at positions driven by onset events, seeding new Turing-pattern growth (spots, stripes, labyrinths) synchronized to the music. — onset -> activator drop at random position (seeds new pattern region); bass -> feed rate F parameter (controls pattern morphology); treble -> kill rate k parameter (shifts between spots and stripes); RMS -> diffusion coefficient ratio (larger = faster pattern spread); beat -> global inhibitor flood that resets a circular region | https://github.com/nicktindall/cyclon.p2p — use https://github.com/pmneila/jsexp (MIT) WebGL Gray-Scott, add audio seeding *(MIT)* |
| 8 | **Sand Mandala Erosion** | ★★★★★ | H | A Tibetan-mandala-like symmetrical pattern of colored CPU sand particles is slowly constructed grain-by-grain during quiet passages. Loud transients trigger erosion events where bass shockwaves scatter sand outward, partially destroying the mandala so it must be rebuilt. — sustained quiet RMS -> construction rate (grains placed per frame along mandala paths); bass onset > threshold -> erosion shockwave radius and scatter velocity; spectral centroid -> sand color hue during construction; beat -> symmetry fold count change (4-fold, 6-fold, 8-fold cycling) | https://github.com/MaxBittker/sandspiel (MIT) — add mandala construction path planner and audio-triggered erosion *(MIT)* |
| 9 | **Boids Flocking Flock** | ★★★★ | M | A CPU/GPU hybrid flock of 1000-4000 boid agents performs classic separation/alignment/cohesion while the audio reshapes their behavioral weights in real time, causing the flock to splinter, merge, and spiral. — bass -> cohesion weight (flock tightness); treble -> separation weight (flock explosiveness); onset -> random scatter impulse applied to all boids; mid energy -> max speed cap; stereo difference -> asymmetric alignment bias | https://github.com/nicktindall/cyclon.p2p — actually use https://github.com/lager1/boids (MIT) or The Coding Train p5.js boids (MIT) *(MIT)* |
| 10 | **Fireworks on Onsets** | ★★★★ | E | Each detected onset fires a firework rocket upward from a random base position; at peak altitude it detonates into a spherical shell burst. Colors and burst radius map to the frequency content of the triggering onset. — onset strength -> burst radius and secondary sparkle count; spectral centroid at onset -> hue of explosion; sub-bass at onset -> rumble ground-shake screen offset; beat regularity -> firework launch cadence predictability | https://github.com/akella/fireworks-webgl (MIT) — add audio-onset triggering layer *(MIT)* |
| 11 | **Spring-Mesh Cloth Bass Ripple** | ★★★★ | H | A subdivided rectangular cloth mesh hangs in 3D, simulated with Verlet spring constraints. Bass transients send shockwave ripples across the fabric; the cloth color maps to local velocity magnitude, glowing bright white at peak displacement. — sub-bass amplitude -> impulse force applied to cloth center node; mid energy -> spring stiffness (tighter cloth = faster ripple decay); treble -> cloth damping coefficient; onset -> pinned corner release that lets cloth fall then re-pins | https://github.com/codementum/clothsim (MIT) — add audio impulse injection and THREE.js rendering *(MIT)* |
| 12 | **Magnetic Field Lines** | ★★★★ | M | Animated field-line streamlines connect pairs of virtual magnetic dipoles. Dipole positions and polarities are modulated by frequency bands, causing field lines to stretch, reconnect, and collapse in musically-timed topological events. — low-mid band -> dipole separation distance; treble -> number of field lines traced; bass onset -> polarity flip event (reconnection); beat -> slow dipole rotation angle; RMS -> glow bloom intensity on lines | from scratch — implement RK4 streamline tracer in WebGL fragment shader using 2-4 dipole superposition |
| 13 | **Granular Sand Pile** | ★★★★ | M | CPU cellular-automaton sand simulation where grains fall and stack. The music continuously rains new grains from above, with color and rain rate driven by the spectrum. Loud transients cause avalanches that shuffle the entire pile. — RMS -> grain rain rate (grains per frame); FFT hue mapping -> grain color by frequency of the triggering bin; onset > threshold -> avalanche (global grain shuffle or tilt event); beat -> periodic crest that sweeps the pile left or right | https://github.com/MaxBittker/sandspiel (MIT) — strip to core sand CA and add audio rain layer *(MIT)* |
| 14 | **Flow Field Ink Drop** | ★★★★ | E | Thousands of CPU particles move through a 2D Perlin-flow field. Each particle leaves a semi-transparent ink trail. The flow field evolves slowly, but audio-reactive noise offsets cause sudden directional surges that look like ink spreading through water. — bass -> flow field z-slice offset (field rotation speed); treble -> particle count added per frame; RMS -> trail opacity (louder = more opaque ink); onset -> flow angle offset jump (+90 degrees) that redirects all particles; spectral centroid -> hue of ink color | https://github.com/nicktindall/cyclon.p2p — use https://github.com/yuichiroharai/glsl-y-noise combined with p5.js flow field example (MIT) *(MIT)* |
| 15 | **Liquid Crystal Defect Annihilation** | ★★★★ | H | A simulated 2D liquid crystal order parameter field evolves via a relaxation PDE on the GPU. Topological defects (+1/2 and -1/2 disclinations) spontaneously form and annihilate. Audio drives the quench rate, creating defect storms on loud passages and smooth textures in quiet sections. — RMS -> thermal noise amplitude injected into the PDE (louder = more defects spawned); bass -> elastic constant (how quickly defects relax); onset -> global quench reset that spawns a defect avalanche; treble -> visualization color palette rotation speed | from scratch — implement Frank elastic free energy PDE in WebGL2 compute shader using ping-pong framebuffers |
| 16 | **Charged Particle Plasma** | ★★★★ | H | Alternating positive and negative point charges repel/attract each other in 2D under Coulomb's law. GPU GPGPU simulation with 8k-32k particles. Audio makes the charge sign flip periodically, generating plasma oscillations and shock fronts. — bass -> overall charge magnitude (stronger = more violent interactions); onset -> sign-flip event for half the particles (creates plasma shock); treble -> particle thermal velocity randomization; RMS -> trail length of each particle; beat -> periodic external electric field pulse that sweeps particles across screen | https://github.com/nicktindall/cyclon.p2p — implement from scratch with WebGL2 GPGPU ping-pong position/velocity textures |
| 17 | **SPH Water Splash** | ★★★★ | H | Smoothed Particle Hydrodynamics (SPH) simulation of 4k-16k fluid particles that form pools and splash walls. Audio transients inject kinetic energy from below, causing the fluid surface to spike, spray droplets, and reform. — sub-bass onset -> upward velocity impulse applied to all particles near the surface; RMS -> external pressure field strength; treble -> surface tension coefficient (high treble = blobby surface); beat -> oscillating tilt of the gravity vector creating slosh | https://github.com/lucasschuermann/sph-tutorial (MIT) — add audio impulse layer and WebGL point-sprite rendering *(MIT)* |
| 18 | **Metaball Blob Swarm** | ★★★★ | M | CPU particles move under mutual attraction while rendering as GPU-raymarched metaballs via a distance-field sum in a fragment shader. Audio causes blobs to merge and split: loud bass merges them into one amoeba, quiet treble breaks them into dozens of tiny droplets. — bass energy -> blob attraction radius (large = merging tendency); treble energy -> repulsion force (small blobs scatter); RMS -> metaball radius scalar; onset -> sudden size spike on all blobs; spectral centroid -> surface color/refraction index | https://www.shadertoy.com/view/ld2GRz (CC BY-NC-SA 3.0) — port to WebGL2, add CPU particle positions as uniforms *(CC BY-NC-SA 3.0)* |
| 19 | **Comet Trail Galaxy Zoom** | ★★★★ | E | Stars are generated as point sprites on a GPU particle system; the camera perpetually zooms forward through the star field at a speed driven by audio intensity, with stars leaving long additive-blend comet trails proportional to their relative velocity. — RMS -> camera forward velocity (louder = faster zoom); bass -> star density in current view frustum; onset -> brief hyperspace jump (extreme velocity spike with motion blur); treble -> star color temperature distribution; beat -> camera slight roll | https://github.com/mrdoob/three.js/blob/dev/examples/webgl_points_sprites.html (MIT) — add audio-reactive velocity and trail length *(MIT)* |
| 20 | **Audio Slitscan Particle Sheet** | ★★★★ | M | A vertical sheet of particles forms a live slitscan: each frame, the current FFT spectrum is extruded into a new column of colored point sprites and pushed backward in Z, building a scrolling 3D history of the spectrum as a physical particle volume. — FFT bin Y-position -> vertical particle position; FFT bin magnitude -> particle brightness and Z-displacement from the sheet plane; spectral centroid over time -> hue rotation of the entire volume; beat -> momentary Z-depth expansion spike | from scratch — Three.js InstancedMesh updated each frame with ring buffer of spectrum history |
| 21 | **Beat-Burst Emitter** | ★★★ | E | On every detected beat or onset, a radial burst of 500-2000 CPU particles explodes from the screen center (or a roaming emitter), each particle inheriting a color from the current spectral centroid and fading out over its lifetime. — onset energy -> particle count and initial velocity magnitude; spectral centroid -> hue of burst; beat period -> emitter cooldown window; RMS amplitude -> particle size at spawn | https://github.com/nicktindall/cyclon.p2p (N/A) — build from scratch using vanilla JS Canvas2D or PixiJS particles |
| 22 | **Fountain Emitter Wall** | ★★★ | M | A horizontal wall of evenly-spaced fountain emitters shoots particles upward. Each column's emitter strength, spray angle, and color is driven by the FFT magnitude of its corresponding frequency band, forming a living audio-reactive curtain. — FFT band power at column X -> emitter velocity for that column; spectral flatness -> spray angle spread (tonal=narrow jet, noisy=wide fan); beat -> global gravity constant pulse; RMS -> particle lifetime | https://github.com/mrdoob/three.js examples/webgl_gpgpu_birds (MIT) — repurpose GPGPU texture as emitter state *(MIT)* |
| 23 | **Constellation Connector** | ★★★ | E | Slow-drifting particles act as stars. When the audio is quiet they drift apart; on louder passages nearby particles (within a dynamic radius) are connected by glowing lines, forming a shifting constellation graph whose density tracks musical intensity. — RMS -> connection radius threshold; bass -> particle drift velocity; treble -> line brightness and flicker rate; onset -> temporary bright flash of all current connections; beat period -> slow hue rotation of the entire field | https://github.com/VincentGarreau/particles.js (MIT) — replace static config with real-time audio parameter injection *(MIT)* |
| 24 | **DNA Helix Particle Wrap** | ★★★ | M | Particles travel along parametric double-helix paths. The helix pitch, radius, and twist rate are continuously modulated by the audio spectrum. Rungs between the two strands flash on beats, and particles shed off the helix on loud transients like ablated material. — bass -> helix radius; treble -> helix pitch (turns per unit length); onset -> rung flash event; spectral centroid -> hue gradient along helix axis; RMS -> number of shed particles per frame; beat -> rotation speed of the entire helix | from scratch — parametric helix math in Three.js with InstancedMesh points |
| 25 | **Verlet Rope Forest** | ★★★ | M | Dozens of Verlet-integrated hanging ropes or chains dangle from top-screen anchor points. Bass hits send transverse waves up each rope; the ropes glow in spectrum-mapped colors and collide softly with each other. — bass amplitude -> impulse force on rope base nodes (larger = wilder swing); each rope index mapped to a frequency band -> glow color and individual excitation; onset -> brief anchor point horizontal displacement; beat -> periodic wind force sweep left to right | https://github.com/subprotocol/verlet-js (MIT) — extend chain constraint type with audio impulse injector *(MIT)* |

Auto-targeting fires at the highest-amplitude frequency peak. Sub-bass creates ICBMs (large, slow, high damage); treble creates fast swarms. Cities survive if the frequency is below silence threshold. — FFT peak positions -> missile X spawn coordinates; peak amplitude -> missile size and damage; sub-bass peak -> ICBM type; treble peaks -> swarm cluster; onset -> auto-fire counter-missile; overall RMS -> incoming missile speed; beat -> simultaneous salvo | from scratch (Canvas 2D) *(from scratch)* |

| 24 | **Onset Snake** | ★★★ | E | A neon Snake that grows one segment on every detected onset. The snake's color cycles through the chromagram (musical key). Heavy bass lanes have fast trucks; silent treble lanes are clear. — 8 octave bands -> 8 lane speeds; onset -> frog hop action; quietest band lane -> frog target lane (auto-pathfind); RMS -> overall traffic density; bass onset -> truck spawn; beat period -> frog hop rhythm | from scratch (Canvas 2D grid) *(from scratch)* |

## 8. Generative & Mathematical Art

| # | Mode | ★ | D | What it does & how it reacts to audio | Adapt from |
|---|------|---|---|----------------------------------------|------------|
| 1 | **Clifford Attractor Field** | ★★★★★ | M | Millions of points iterated through the Clifford attractor form a dense, shifting cloud of light. The four attractor parameters are smoothly mapped to audio bands so the shape morphs continuously. — sub-bass -> parameter a; bass -> parameter b; mid -> parameter c; treble -> parameter d (all interpolated in range [-2, 2]); RMS -> point count / density; beat onset -> brief parameter jump then smooth return; brightness -> log-scaled point density per pixel | https://github.com/timhutton/clifford-attractor (MIT) *(MIT)* |
| 2 | **Gray-Scott Reaction-Diffusion** | ★★★★★ | H | Two virtual chemicals diffuse and react across the canvas, forming spots, stripes, spirals, and labyrinths. Audio shifts the feed and kill rates, causing the system to abruptly reorganize into a different pattern family. — bass energy -> feed rate F (range 0.01–0.08); treble energy -> kill rate k (range 0.04–0.07); beat onset -> local disturbance injection (chemical B spike at beat-reactive position); RMS -> diffusion rate scalar; spectral centroid -> color palette selection (viridis/magma/cool) | https://github.com/pmneila/jsexp (MIT) *(MIT)* |
| 3 | **Perlin Flow Field** | ★★★★★ | M | Thousands of particles follow vector fields derived from layered Perlin noise. The noise offsets scroll through time with the music, and particles leave glowing trails that accumulate into an oil-paint texture. — bass energy -> noise scale (zoom level of the field); treble energy -> particle speed scalar; beat onset -> noise seed jump (field reorganizes); RMS -> number of active particles; spectral centroid -> trail color hue; mid energy -> noise lacunarity / octave count; overall loudness -> trail opacity and fade rate | https://github.com/jwagner/simplex-noise.js (MIT) for noise; rendering from scratch *(MIT)* |
| 4 | **Iterated Function System (IFS) Flame** | ★★★★★ | H | An IFS chaos-game renders fractal flames whose affine transform weights are biased by frequency bands, causing the flame to morph between fern, spiral, and abstract attractor shapes in real time. — N frequency bands -> probability weights of N IFS transforms; overall RMS -> gamma correction (brightness/contrast); beat onset -> randomize variation type of one transform; spectral centroid -> hue rotation of log-density color map; treble -> final transform zoom | https://github.com/scottdraves/flam3 (GPL) — port core iteration to WebGL *(GPL)* |
| 5 | **Lenia Continuous Cellular Automaton** | ★★★★★ | H | Lenia, the continuous generalization of Game of Life, produces life-like moving creatures called 'gliders'. Audio perturbs the kernel growth function, causing creatures to multiply, die, or transform. — bass energy -> growth function mu (center of bell curve); treble energy -> sigma (width of bell curve); RMS -> time step delta-t (simulation speed); beat onset -> inject a known glider pattern at a random position; spectral centroid -> color map selection; mid energy -> kernel radius | https://github.com/Chakazul/Lenia (MIT) *(MIT)* |
| 6 | **Menger Sponge / 3D Fractal Ray-March** | ★★★★★ | H | A ray-marched 3D Menger sponge (or Mandelbox) rotates in space. Audio controls the folding scale and iteration count, letting the structure breathe between full fractal complexity and a simple cube. — bass RMS -> scale parameter (Mandelbox folding scale, range 1.5–3.0); treble RMS -> iteration depth (2–8); beat onset -> camera orbit impulse; mid energy -> ambient occlusion strength; spectral centroid -> diffuse color hue; RMS -> glow intensity of surface emission; loudness -> fog density | https://www.shadertoy.com/view/XdBGzd (MIT / Shadertoy) *(MIT)* |
| 7 | **Falling Sand / Powder Toy** | ★★★★ | M | Particles of sand, water, fire, and smoke fall and interact. Beats spawn bursts of fire or sand from the top; bass shakes gravity sideways; treble creates upward wind currents that scatter particles. — beat onset -> particle burst type cycles (sand/fire/water/smoke); bass magnitude -> horizontal gravity offset; treble band -> upward wind force; RMS -> spawn rate; spectral centroid -> particle hue (low=orange, high=blue) | https://github.com/MaxBittker/sandspiel (MIT) *(MIT)* |
| 8 | **Lorenz Strange Attractor** | ★★★★ | M | A particle traces the iconic butterfly-shaped Lorenz attractor in 3D, rendered as a glowing ribbon trail. Audio modulates the sigma, rho, and beta parameters, pushing the system in and out of chaotic regimes. — bass RMS -> rho parameter (28 ± 12); mid RMS -> sigma parameter (10 ± 4); treble RMS -> beta parameter (2.67 ± 1); beat onset -> camera orbit impulse; overall loudness -> trail length and glow radius; spectral centroid -> hue rotation along trail | https://www.shadertoy.com/view/llsfR4 (Shadertoy default/CC BY-NC-SA) *(CC BY-NC-SA)* |
| 9 | **De Jong Attractor Bloom** | ★★★★ | E | The Peter de Jong attractor renders as a soft luminous cloud that morphs between radically different shapes as its four parameters respond to different frequency bands, producing organic, flower-like forms. — four frequency sub-bands (0-200Hz, 200-800Hz, 800-3kHz, 3k+Hz) each control one of the four de Jong parameters a/b/c/d; RMS -> iteration count per frame; beat onset -> hue phase shift; spectral flatness -> color desaturation (noise=grey, tonal=vivid) | from scratch (standard 2D iteration; ~50 lines of JS/WebGL) |
| 10 | **Voronoi Shimmer** | ★★★★ | M | A Voronoi diagram computed from moving seed points covers the canvas in glowing cells. Seeds drift with Perlin noise; beats fire new seeds into the field; each cell's color encodes its distance metric modulated by frequency. — beat onset -> spawn 3-8 new seeds with kick velocity; bass RMS -> seed drift speed; treble band -> cell border glow width; per-cell hue -> mapped to distance from nearest seed modulated by mid-frequency amplitude; spectral centroid -> global hue rotation; loudness -> cell brightness | https://www.shadertoy.com/view/llG3zy (Inigo Quilez, MIT) *(MIT)* |
| 11 | **Delaunay Triangulation Mesh** | ★★★★ | M | A triangulated mesh of points pulses and shifts. Each triangle's fill color encodes local audio energy at its centroid frequency. | 12 | **Phyllotaxis Spiral (Sunflower)** | ★★★★ | E | Dots arranged in the golden-angle sunflower spiral pulse outward and change size with the beat. New dots spiral in continuously; each ring corresponds to a frequency bin. — radial position of each dot -> mapped to frequency bin (center=bass, edge=treble); dot radius -> amplitude of its frequency bin; beat onset -> outward pulse wave (all radii expand then contract); overall RMS -> rotation speed of the entire pattern; dot hue -> frequency bin index mapped through HSL spectrum | from scratch (20 lines: angle = i * 137.508°, r = c * sqrt(i)) |
| 13 | **Chladni Plate (Modal Frequency Patterns)** | ★★★★ | M | Simulates sand accumulating at the nodal lines of a vibrating plate. The plate's resonant mode (m, n integers) is selected by the dominant frequency, producing the iconic geometric sand figures that change with each note. — dominant pitch frequency -> selects (m, n) mode pair that maps to that pitch; RMS amplitude -> sand density / brightness of nodal lines; beat onset -> plate excitation impulse (brief flash); spectral flux -> blend between two adjacent modes; sub-bass -> full-plate low-frequency shudder animation | from scratch (sin(m*pi*x)*sin(n*pi*y) nodal equation; ~60 lines) |
| 14 | **Harmonograph Pendulum** | ★★★★ | E | Simulates two coupled pendulums drawing Lissajous-like figures. The frequency ratios of the pendulums are tuned to detected pitches, and damping is tied to the decay of notes, drawing beautiful spirograph patterns. — detected pitch 1 -> pendulum 1 frequency ratio; detected pitch 2 -> pendulum 2 frequency ratio; RMS -> initial amplitude of both pendulums; note release decay -> damping coefficient (notes decay = lines spiral inward); beat onset -> restart pendulum with new amplitude; treble -> line color hue; bass -> x-phase offset | from scratch (parametric x=A*sin(at+d), y=B*sin(bt); ~30 lines) |
| 15 | **Superformula Bloom** | ★★★★ | E | Johan Gielis's superformula generates a single 2D shape that continuously morphs between radically different forms — stars, flowers, polygons, amoebae — as audio modulates its six parameters. — m (symmetry order) -> quantized to 3-12 based on detected tempo subdivision; a, b -> scaled by bass and treble RMS; n1, n2, n3 -> mapped to low-mid, mid, high-mid frequency bands; beat onset -> sudden parameter jump then smooth lerp back; overall loudness -> shape scale; rotation speed -> BPM / 60 | from scratch (r = superformula(theta, m, n1, n2, n3, a, b); ~40 lines) |
| 16 | **Mandelbrot / Julia Set Explorer** | ★★★★ | M | The complex parameter c of a Julia set is animated along the Mandelbrot boundary by audio, morphing between connected and disconnected fractal forms. Zoom depth and color cycling respond to amplitude. — bass energy -> real component of c (Re); treble energy -> imaginary component of c (Im); RMS -> zoom level (louder = deeper zoom); spectral centroid -> color cycling speed; beat onset -> camera position jump to a new Mandelbrot boundary point; mid energy -> max iteration count | https://www.shadertoy.com/view/lsX3W4 (public domain / CC0) *(CC0)* |
| 17 | **Turing Pattern Morphogenesis** | ★★★★ | H | Alan Turing's 1952 reaction-diffusion morphogenesis model produces spots and stripes reminiscent of animal coats. Audio controls the activator/inhibitor diffusion ratio, shifting between spot patterns (leopard) and stripe patterns (zebra). — bass/treble ratio -> diffusion ratio Da/Db (controls spot vs stripe regime); RMS -> reaction rate constant; beat onset -> inject activator pulse at a random canvas region; mid energy -> grid resolution; spectral flatness -> noise added to initial conditions; loudness -> color contrast | https://github.com/nicktindall/cyclon.p2p (from scratch — reference: Karl Sims RD; ~80 lines GLSL) |
| 18 | **Perlin Terrain (Topographic Scan)** | ★★★★ | M | A scrolling 3D terrain mesh generated with Perlin noise is viewed from a low angle. Bass shapes low-frequency hills; treble adds high-frequency rocky detail; the terrain scrolls forward at tempo. — bass band -> low-frequency noise octave amplitude (hill height); treble band -> high-frequency noise octave amplitude (rock texture); tempo BPM -> terrain scroll speed; beat onset -> color palette shift and brief height spike at horizon; RMS -> overall terrain exaggeration; spectral centroid -> fog color hue | https://github.com/jwagner/terrain (MIT) or Three.js PlaneGeometry + simplex noise *(MIT)* |
| 19 | **Conway's Game of Life (Beat-Seeded)** | ★★★ | E | A classic cellular automaton where new live cells are seeded at beat onsets and the colony evolves at a rate tied to tempo. | 20 | **L-System Fractal Tree** | ★★★ | M | A branching fractal tree grows outward in real time. Each beat adds a new generation of branches; amplitude controls branch length; the tree sways rhythmically with low-frequency energy. — beat onset -> increment L-system generation (tree grows); RMS amplitude -> branch segment length scalar; bass energy -> trunk sway angle; mid energy -> branching angle spread; treble energy -> leaf opacity and color saturation; spectral flux -> random mutation of production rules | https://github.com/nicktindall/cyclon.p2p (from scratch — standard L-system is trivial; reference: Paul Bourke's L-system page) |

Tiles near frequency-reactive grid zones flip with each beat, and the color scheme pulses. — beat onset -> random tile-flip cascade radius from center; bass energy -> flip probability per tile per frame; treble energy -> tile size (zoom in/out); RMS -> color brightness; spectral rolloff frequency -> hue of arc vs background; mid band -> stroke weight | from scratch (trivial arc-drawing; reference: The Coding Train Truchet sketch CC0) *(CC0)* |
| 22 | **Barnsley Fern & IFS Variations** | ★★★ | E | The Barnsley fern chaos game runs in real time; each of its four affine transforms has its selection probability nudged by a different frequency band, causing the fern to sprout extra leaflets or shrink into a stem. — 4 frequency sub-bands -> 4 IFS transform probabilities (normalized to sum to 1); RMS -> points per frame; beat onset -> color flash and brief probability spike on transform 4 (frond); treble -> point size; overall loudness -> rotation of the entire fern; spectral centroid -> hue shift | from scratch (classic 4-transform IFS; ~30 lines) |
