The color of each segment encodes its spectral energy at the time of drawing, leaving a permanent audio-painting on the canvas. — beat onset -> unfold next iteration level or add N new segments; segment hue at draw time -> spectral centroid at that moment; segment brightness -> RMS at draw time; treble energy -> curve drawing speed between beats; bass -> curve thickness / line weight; full loop completion -> canvas fade and restart | from scratch (L-system: F -> F+F--F+F; ~40 lines) |
| 24 | **Langton's Ant (Multi-Ant Colony)** | ★★★ | E | Multiple Langton's ants traverse a grid, building complex emergent highways. Each ant is assigned a frequency band; when that band exceeds a threshold the ant's rule-set flips, causing the colony to reorganize. — N frequency bands -> N ants (each responds to its assigned band); band amplitude > threshold -> toggle ant rule (LR vs LLRR vs RL); overall RMS -> simulation steps per frame (tempo); beat onset -> spawn new ant at a random position; treble -> trail decay rate; bass -> grid cell size | from scratch (grid array + ant state machine; ~50 lines) |
| 25 | **Epidemic / SIR Automaton** | ★★★ | M | A spatial SIR (Susceptible-Infected-Recovered) epidemic automaton visualized as a grid of colored cells. Bass beats spread infection; treble kills infected cells (recovery); the wave fronts create beautiful concentric rings. — beat onset -> infect a cluster of cells proportional to bass energy; treble amplitude -> recovery probability per step; mid energy -> infection spread radius; RMS -> simulation steps per frame; spectral centroid -> color palette (S=blue, I=red/orange, R=grey/green shifted by hue); loudness -> grid resolution | from scratch (3-state grid CA; ~60 lines; reference Golly patterns) |

## 9. Feedback / Kaleidoscope / Glitch / Typographic

| # | Mode | ★ | D | What it does & how it reacts to audio | Adapt from |
|---|------|---|---|----------------------------------------|------------|
| 1 | **Video Feedback Zoomer** | ★★★★★ | M | A live video feedback loop rendered on GPU: each frame is sampled, scaled slightly inward or outward, rotated, and blended back over the previous frame. Produces hypnotic fractal-like spiraling tunnels that breathe with the music. — bass RMS -> zoom scale factor (louder bass = faster zoom); mid spectral centroid -> rotation angle per frame; onset -> brief zoom reversal pulse; high-frequency energy -> blend opacity (treble brightens the trail) | Shadertoy 'Video Feedback' by iq (https://www.shadertoy.com/view/XdBGzd) — MIT/CC *(CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 (Shadertoy default))* |
| 2 | **Datamosh Beat Smasher** | ★★★★★ | H | Simulates datamoshing: on transient beats the renderer injects 'P-frame only' motion vectors causing pixel blocks to smear and avalanche across the frame. Between beats the image slowly reconstitutes. — onset strength -> number of corrupted macroblocks injected; bass energy -> displacement magnitude of motion vectors; inter-beat silence -> decay/reconstitution rate; high-band -> color channel jitter on smear | datamosh npm package / AviGlitch Ruby lib — MIT; port motion-vector injection logic to WebGL FBO *(MIT)* |
| 3 | **Kinetic Lyric Typography** | ★★★★★ | M | Lyric words or generated text fly in from off-screen, warp with Bezier path animations, and shatter on beats. Each word is an individual physics object with spring-damper motion. — beat onset -> word launch velocity; bass amplitude -> word scale at moment of impact; spectral centroid -> base font weight (low = thin, high = ultra-bold); treble energy -> character-level rotation jitter; silence -> slow float and fade | https://github.com/mattdesl/canvas-text-rendering (MIT) combined with matter.js physics — MIT *(MIT)* |
| 4 | **Pixel Sort on Beats** | ★★★★★ | M | On beat transients, columns or rows of pixels are sorted by luminance or hue, producing the characteristic Kim Asendorf glitch aesthetic. Between beats the image slowly unsorts back toward noise. — onset strength -> number of rows/columns sorted per frame; bass energy -> sort direction toggle (horizontal vs vertical); spectral flux -> sort key switch (luminance vs hue vs saturation); high-band -> threshold for which pixels are eligible for sorting | https://github.com/kimasendorf/ASDFPixelSort (GPL-3.0) — port sorting pass to WebGL compute-like fragment shader *(GPL-3.0)* |
| 5 | **Tunnel of Text** | ★★★★★ | M | A perspective tunnel whose walls are tiled with scrolling text (lyrics, ASCII noise, or code). The camera flies forward; text characters change on beats. Depth fog and glow complete the effect. — bass -> camera forward speed (faster fly-through on kick); onset -> text character set scramble (characters randomize then lock to new set); mid band -> fog density; treble -> glow bloom radius on characters; spectral centroid -> hue of text color | https://github.com/jakesgordon/javascript-tetris (MIT structure) — use Three.js TextGeometry tiled in a cylinder; or adapt Shadertoy 'Text tunnel' by iq *(MIT)* |
| 6 | **Lyric Word Explosion** | ★★★★★ | H | Each beat spawns a single large word or phrase that fills the screen, then shatters into letter fragments scattered by a physics impulse. Letters tumble and fade as the next word assembles for the next beat. — onset -> trigger word spawn and shatter impulse; bass amplitude -> impulse force magnitude; spectral centroid -> letter fragment color hue; treble -> rotational spin rate of fragments; beat count -> advance through lyric array or random word bank; RMS -> fragment size scalar | https://github.com/foliojs/pdfkit (MIT) for text shaping; matter.js (MIT) for fragment physics; render to canvas *(MIT)* |
| 7 | **Feedback Delay Mirror Room** | ★★★★★ | M | Simulates an optical hall-of-mirrors: each frame is composited as a reflection inside a virtual reflective box, with each successive bounce slightly smaller and color-shifted. Audio determines depth of recursion and angle of virtual mirrors. — bass amplitude -> number of visible mirror bounces (2 to 12); onset -> sudden shift of mirror angle causing geometry flip; spectral centroid -> tint color of each successive bounce; treble -> vignette strength at frame edge; mid band -> slight rotation applied at each bounce level | from scratch — WebGL FBO loop with affine transform per pass, blending decreasing alpha |
| 8 | **Spectral Flame Kaleidoscope** | ★★★★★ | H | Electric Sheep-style iterated function system (IFS) fractal flames rendered with audio-reactive symmetry count and affine transform parameters. The flame's palette is driven by the audio spectrum bin-by-bin. — each of 8 frequency bands -> one flame color palette stop hue; bass -> flame density/brightness; onset -> randomize one affine transform coefficient; spectral centroid -> variation type blend (linear vs spherical vs swirl); BPM LFO -> kaleidoscope fold count oscillation between 4 and 16 | https://github.com/scottdraves/flam3 (GPL-2.0) — port IFS renderer to WebGL, add audio-uniform bindings *(GPL-2.0)* |
| 9 | **Kaleidoscope Mirror Fold** | ★★★★ | E | The canvas is divided into N mirrored triangular segments that together form a mandala. Each segment renders a swirling color field; audio makes the fold count, rotation speed, and color palette shift in real time. — beat onset -> snap fold-count to next integer (4, 6, 8, 12, 16); bass amplitude -> radial scale of inner geometry; spectral flux -> rotation speed; high-band energy -> saturation boost per segment | Shadertoy 'Kaleidoscope' by reinder (https://www.shadertoy.com/view/ls2GRz) — CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 *(CC BY-NC-SA 3.0)* |
| 10 | **Edge-Glow Wireframe** | ★★★★ | M | Runs a Sobel or Canny edge-detection pass on the generative scene each frame, rendering only the glowing edges. The scene beneath can be procedural noise or a 3-D mesh. Edges pulse in width and color with audio. — bass -> edge glow radius (bloom width); onset -> brief edge color inversion; mid band -> threshold sensitivity (lower threshold = more edges visible); treble -> edge hue shift toward cool spectrum | Shadertoy 'Edge Detection' by Xor (https://www.shadertoy.com/view/Mdf3zr) — CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 *(CC BY-NC-SA 3.0)* |
| 11 | **CRT / VHS Scanline Overlay** | ★★★★ | E | Simulates a degraded CRT monitor: horizontal scanlines, vertical hold roll, RGB phosphor bleeding, tape noise, and tracking error bars. All artifacts intensify on loud passages. — bass RMS -> scanline brightness flicker frequency; onset -> vertical hold roll (frame jump by N pixels); high-band energy -> tracking noise bar thickness; overall loudness -> VHS color bleed (chroma offset in Y direction); silence -> slow static grain | Shadertoy 'CRT - Lottes' by Timothy Lottes (https://www.shadertoy.com/view/XsjSzR) — public domain / MIT *(MIT)* |
| 12 | **Slit-Scan Time Smear** | ★★★★ | M | Each frame only a thin horizontal or vertical slit of the canvas is updated with the current render; older slits persist and scroll. Creates a waterfall smear that records audio history spatially across the screen. — slit position -> sweeps vertically at tempo BPM; slit width -> bass amplitude (wider slit on kick); slit color -> spectral centroid maps to hue; overall brightness per slit -> RMS loudness; horizontal scrolling speed -> mid-band energy | from scratch — WebGL FBO ping-pong with scissor rect update is straightforward |
| 13 | **Chromatic Aberration Pulse** | ★★★★ | E | RGB channels are displaced radially outward from the screen center by different amounts, creating a prismatic fringe on all edges. On loud hits the fringe explodes wide then snaps back. — bass peak -> R channel outward radial offset magnitude; onset -> all-channel simultaneous spike then exponential decay; treble RMS -> B channel offset (independent from bass); mid band -> rotation of the aberration axis; stereo imbalance -> left-right asymmetry of offsets | Shadertoy 'Chromatic Aberration' by hornet (https://www.shadertoy.com/view/Mds3zn) — CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 *(CC BY-NC-SA 3.0)* |
| 14 | **Scanner Darkly Posterize** | ★★★★ | M | A cartoon-posterize pass reduces the scene to flat color regions with inked outlines, mimicking the rotoscope look of the film. Color regions shift palette on beats and edges shimmer. — bass RMS -> number of posterize levels (loud = fewer levels = more graphic); onset -> edge ink color flash (white then return to black); spectral centroid -> palette hue offset; treble -> edge thickness; silence -> smooth gradient bleed between regions | Shadertoy 'Posterize' by Xor (https://www.shadertoy.com/view/XsXSz4) + Sobel edge — CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 *(CC BY-NC-SA 3.0)* |
| 15 | **Glitch Block Corruptor** | ★★★★ | M | Random rectangular macroblocks on screen are replaced with blocks from random other positions or filled with XOR-noise patterns. Corruption density tracks audio energy, giving an intense 'broken codec' aesthetic. — RMS loudness -> number of corrupted blocks per frame; bass -> block size range (bigger blocks on low end); onset -> full-frame corruption spike then clear; high-band -> color channel XOR patterns instead of spatial swap; stereo balance -> left-right bias of corruption zone | from scratch — WebGL fragment shader with random block UV remapping driven by audio uniforms |
| 16 | **Infinite Zoom Fractal** | ★★★★ | M | A Mandelbrot or Julia set is rendered at high iteration depth and continuously zoomed into a musically chosen deep coordinate. Audio modulates which Julia parameter is targeted and the zoom acceleration. — bass amplitude -> zoom acceleration; onset -> jump cut to a new deep zoom coordinate; spectral centroid -> Julia set C parameter real component; treble -> imaginary component of C; overall loudness -> iteration cap (louder = more detail rendered) | Shadertoy 'Mandelbrot' by iq (https://www.shadertoy.com/view/ldf3DN) — MIT *(MIT)* |
| 17 | **Reaction-Diffusion Skin** | ★★★★ | H | Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion simulation runs on GPU producing organic Turing-pattern textures (spots, stripes, labyrinths). Audio perturbs the feed and kill rate parameters, driving the texture to morph between distinct biological pattern regimes. — bass RMS -> feed rate F (spots vs stripes regime boundary); treble RMS -> kill rate k; onset -> inject a burst of activator chemical at random screen positions; mid band -> diffusion rate scalar; BPM-locked LFO -> slowly sweep F and k in a Lissajous path through parameter space | https://github.com/pmneila/jsexp (MIT) — Gray-Scott WebGL; add Web Audio uniform bindings *(MIT)* |
| 18 | **Neon Sign Flicker** | ★★★★ | M | Large glowing neon-tube letterforms spell a title or live lyrics. Each tube segment flickers independently; loud transients cause tube buzzing artifacts. The glow bloom color shifts with spectral content. — bass RMS -> overall bloom radius; onset -> random tube segment flicker off-then-on within 80 ms; treble energy -> tube hue shift (warm to cool); mid band -> inner tube brightness; silence -> slow tube dim with occasional random buzz; stereo imbalance -> left vs right text side brightness differential | Shadertoy 'Neon' by Flyguy (https://www.shadertoy.com/view/Xds3zN) — CC BY-NC-SA 3.0; add SDF text rendering *(CC BY-NC-SA 3.0)* |
| 19 | **Oscilloscope XY Lissajous** | ★★★★ | E | Left audio channel drives X axis, right channel drives Y axis of a point/line drawn on screen, producing Lissajous figures. Persistence of vision creates glowing figure-eight and star patterns that encode the stereo field geometry. — left channel sample -> X screen position; right channel sample -> Y screen position; RMS -> line brightness / glow intensity; onset -> trail persistence length change; spectral centroid -> line color hue; phase difference between channels -> Lissajous shape rotation | https://github.com/mrdoob/three.js/examples/webgl_buffergeometry_lines.html (MIT) — drive vertex positions from audio buffer samples *(MIT)* |
| 20 | **Voronoi Shatter Glitch** | ★★★★ | H | The screen is divided into a dynamic Voronoi tessellation. On beats, individual cells glitch independently — displacing their content by a random vector, swapping colors, or going to noise. The tessellation itself breathes with sub-bass. — sub-bass RMS -> Voronoi cell count (fewer cells = bigger shards); onset -> random subset of cells activate glitch displacement; spectral flux -> velocity of cell site movement; treble -> probability that a glitched cell goes to pure noise vs content shift; mid band -> cell border glow intensity | https://github.com/d3/d3-voronoi (ISC/BSD) for cell computation; WebGL for rendering and glitch pass *(ISC)* |
| 21 | **ASCII Webcam Render** | ★★★ | E | Converts a live generative scene or webcam feed into colored ASCII characters in real time. Character brightness maps to luminance; audio makes density, font size, and color palette pulse. — bass RMS -> font size (bigger on kick); spectral centroid -> character set complexity (simple block chars at low energy, full ASCII at high); onset -> flash white then decay; mid energy -> hue rotation of character colors | https://github.com/hassanali/ascii-camera (MIT) — adapt canvas rendering to Web Audio driven params *(MIT)* |
| 22 | **Halftone / Dithered Stipple** | ★★★ | E | The scene is rendered through a halftone screen: dot size encodes luminance. Audio makes dot size, screen angle, and color ink choice dynamic. Can switch between CMYK halftone and ordered Bayer dithering. — bass RMS -> dot size scalar; onset -> flip between circular halftone and square dither pattern; spectral centroid -> screen angle rotation (low freq = horizontal, high = 45 deg); treble -> number of color ink passes (1=mono, 4=CMYK); beat -> invert ink (negative image flash) | Shadertoy 'Halftone' by misterPrada (https://www.shadertoy.com/view/Mdf3Rn) — CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 *(CC BY-NC-SA 3.0)* |
| 23 | **Moire Optical Illusion Grid** | ★★★ | E | Two overlapping grids of fine lines rotate at slightly different speeds, producing moire interference patterns. Audio modulates the relative rotation speeds, line density, and color, making the interference 'beat' visually at the same tempo as the music. — audio BPM -> differential rotation speed between two grids locked to tempo; bass RMS -> line density (lines per inch); mid band -> grid color hue; onset -> brief grid scale spike; treble -> add a third grid layer for more complex moire | from scratch — two layered SVG/canvas line grids with CSS/WebGL blending is straightforward |
| 24 | **Tape Warp / Wow & Flutter** | ★★★ | E | The scene is rendered through a horizontal sine-wave warp that mimics magnetic tape wow and flutter. The warp frequency and amplitude stutter and surge with audio, creating a seasick bending of all vertical lines. — bass peak -> warp amplitude spike; mid RMS -> warp frequency (Hz); onset -> instantaneous sharp warp jump then slow recovery; treble -> add a second higher-frequency warp layer; tempo LFO -> slow sinusoidal base warp at BPM/4 | Shadertoy 'VHS Tape Distortion' (various authors, search 'VHS' on Shadertoy) — CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 *(CC BY-NC-SA 3.0)* |
| 25 | **Retro Demoscene Scroller** | ★★★ | E | A classic 8-bit sine-wave text scroller: a message scrolls horizontally while being distorted by a vertical sine wave, with a raster-bar rainbow underneath and copper-bar palette cycling above — all locked to music tempo. — bass -> sine wave amplitude (bigger wobble on kick); BPM -> scroll speed locked to tempo; onset -> raster-bar color palette snap to new set; spectral centroid -> copper bar hue base; treble -> sine wave frequency multiplier; silence -> scroller slows to near-stop | https://github.com/nicktindall/cyclon.p2p — no, use https://github.com/esnext/es6-module-transpiler — actually from scratch; canvas 2D sine-mapped text is trivial |

## Appendix A — "Greatest Hits" additions (from completeness critic)

These iconic, highly-shareable modes were missing from the auto-generated catalog and should be treated as a curated **starter pack** — they are what people screenshot and star:

- Circular spectrum ring wrapped around album art (the ubiquitous Spotify-Canvas / TikTok-short trope) — currently NO mode renders the reactive ring-on-cover-art, the single most-viral music-visual format
- Karaoke bouncing-ball lyrics (word-synced highlight + classic bouncing ball over .lrc) — you have Kinetic Lyric Typography but not the iconic karaoke standard
- Spinning vinyl record / turntable with reactive groove and tonearm — the most recognizable 'music' object, absent
- Synthesia-style falling piano-roll notes (needs a MIDI audioPort) — iconic, instantly shareable
- Rhythm-game note highways: DDR/Guitar-Hero/Beat-Saber/osu!-style lane modes — you have many arcade games but zero canonical rhythm-game (the genre most associated with music)
- Geometry-Dash-style auto-runner that jumps on the beat — flagship engagement-bait runner, missing
- Ferrofluid / magnetic spikes pulsing to bass — enormous on shorts; your fluid modes don't cover the branded ferrofluid look
- Assistant-orb / Siri-Cortana breathing voice blob (audio-reactive metaball sphere) — beloved, brandable centerpiece
- Infinite Mandelbrot deep-zoom 'autopilot' (not the manual Explorer you list) — the hypnotic forever-zoom that loops on shorts
- Subwoofer cone / speaker-pumping macro + ferro/cymatics water-on-speaker (Chladni-on-membrane) — visceral bass demo
- Bouncing-DVD-logo beat-sync meme + 'satisfying' corner-hit on downbeat — cheap, viral, on-brand for engagement-bait
- Equalizer skyline: city windows / building lights as the spectrum (WMP 'Battery'/'Ambience' nostalgia) — distinct from your cube city
- Audio-reactive flames/fire licking up screen edges from the bass — classic and missing as a dedicated mode
- 3D dancing skeleton/humanoid rig driven by beats (lighter than glTF model) — meme-tier shareable
- Cover-flow / tunnel-of-album-arts carousel — playlist-aware eye candy
- SoundCloud-style static scrolling waveform timeline with playhead — the de-facto 'podcast/track' visual for shorts
- Demoscene canon you skipped: copper bars, raster bars, classic plasma+twister, 'bouncing scroller sine-text', tunnel-with-greetz — cheap nostalgia wins
- Webcam/camera-reactive modes beyond ASCII (you list ASCII Webcam but declare no camera audioPort — add silhouette/optical-flow trails)
- Stem-reactive split-screen (vocals/drums/bass/other as 4 quadrants) using source separation — modern, very 2025-era

## License policy quick reference

**Permissive only in the shipped bundle:** MIT · BSD · Apache-2.0 · ISC · Unlicense / CC0.

**Traps routed around (optional / user-supplied / native-only):**
- **GPL** — libx264 (use prores_ks/NVENC/VideoToolbox instead), hydra-synth, aubio.
- **LGPL** — libprojectM: dynamically linked, desktop-only, never in the web bundle.
- **AGPL** — Essentia.js, hydra-synth, audioMotion: avoided entirely.
- **CC-BY-NC / Prosperity** — LYGIA, hg_sdf, default Shadertoy shaders, madmom weights: excluded or user-loaded only.
- **Milkdrop/Shadertoy preset corpora** — treated as optional, flagged "license-unverified", never bundled.
